114085.explanation: Subsidies are already available for this. Lokaal+ believes the municipality can also help in other ways, such as with practical tips and permits.

114088.explanation: Lokaal+ agrees. There is a severe housing shortage. Work is often location-bound. Living nearby is practical. This helps prevent nuisance and illegal housing. If you need people to do the work, you must also ensure they have proper places to sleep.

114090.explanation: Lokaal+ does not want a total ban. A burning ban works if it is temporary during code red or smog. In those cases, Lokaal+ finds careful enforcement and clear explanations important. Additionally, the municipality should provide good information about responsible burning.

114094.explanation: For Lokaal+, the current noise regulations for parties and festivals are sufficient.

114095.explanation: Accessible buildings are important. This is not only the responsibility of the municipality but also of the entrepreneurs themselves. Toegankelijk Geertruidenberg can play an important role for entrepreneurs in this.

114100.explanation: Lokaal+ believes schools should decide themselves what to spend extra money on. Music lessons can be included but need not be a fixed arrangement. There is already a scheme for children of residents receiving social assistance.

114102.explanation: Lokaal+ opposes cuts in monument care. Our fortress city Geertruidenberg has a beautiful monumental and tourist core. Our heritage-including in other districts-is important and must be preserved for future generations.

114103.explanation: BOA officers contribute to safety. Lokaal+ wants to employ more BOA officers.

114104.explanation: Camera surveillance works preventively and contributes to safety and investigation. Therefore, Lokaal+ supports more camera surveillance. However, Lokaal+ values careful implementation, clear objectives, and good control.

114109.explanation: There are no holiday parks in our municipality. The future use of the former Hermenzeil campsite is still unclear. As long as the housing shortage is great, Lokaal+ is principledly not opposed to using holiday homes as permanent residences.

114110.explanation: Mandatory socially useful work is legally not allowed for people with benefits. Lokaal+ is therefore not in favor of this. However, the municipality should encourage people with benefits to volunteer.

114111.explanation: For these residents, these taxes often constitute a heavy burden. Remission helps prevent people from falling into financial problems. This should be part of a broader support program. Every resident should be able to access help and guidance from the municipality, regardless of income.

114112.explanation: There are provisions to assist people with incomes below the income threshold. If the threshold is raised, assistance also goes to residents who can manage on their own better. Lokaal+ believes help should therefore be intended for those who really need it.

114114.explanation: There must be a minimum number of places. Moreover, being able to charge electric vehicles is primarily one's own responsibility.

114115.explanation: 30 km/h within built-up areas makes traffic safer. Lokaal+ believes several roads should be adjusted to this speed.

114116.explanation: Lokaal+ agrees with the freedom to choose. It is legally regulated that people have a choice. Sometimes this freedom means people have to pay extra themselves if the choice is not within regular providers.

114117.explanation: Lokaal+ believes our youth deserve high-quality help, which is therefore the first priority when selecting organizations. For Lokaal+, quality is more important than the lowest price.

114118.explanation: With the property tax (OZB) we pay for important facilities. Only well-justified changes to increase or decrease the OZB are acceptable to Lokaal+.

114120.explanation: Lokaal+ wants to stimulate tourism in our municipality. An increase in tourist tax will not help in the coming years.

114128.explanation: Young children (up to 6 years old) often already have a swimming diploma. For Lokaal+, school swimming is therefore not needed. There is already a scheme for children of residents receiving social assistance.

114130.explanation: This is already the case in our municipality. We must comply with the regional agreement of 30% social rental housing.

114132.explanation: There has been a housing shortage for years. The ban on renting by room worsens the housing crisis and restricts housing seekers' freedom of choice. Policy must be developed to determine where and under what conditions-such as fire safety and nuisance prevention-room rental is permitted.

114133.explanation: More parking spaces in neighborhoods reduce livability. Livability is not only about parking but also about greenery and foot and bike paths. Lokaal+ believes we should use the available space more intelligently. Furthermore, investing in alternatives is more sustainable and effective.

114136.explanation: Artificial intelligence is a useful tool that the municipality of Geertruidenberg can use. With AI, we can work faster, produce better documents, and process information more efficiently. Decisions about people should remain human.

114137.explanation: As long as the municipality can remain independent, it should do so. We are in favor of cooperation with other municipalities if it improves service delivery to our residents.

114138.explanation: Lokaal+ considers citizen participation an important principle for making decisions. Young people must also be able to have a say. A youth council can help with this.

114139.explanation: It is important to consider all options for (additional) housing development. Therefore, Lokaal+ is not opposed to possible construction east of the A27.

114085.explanation: The municipality encourages the establishment of an official body, such as an energy cooperative, to prevent uncontrolled growth. They organize this together with residents.

114088.explanation: To provide suitable housing for labor migrants, it is very important that employers have the opportunity to create living quarters on their own company premises, as part of good employment practices.

114090.explanation: Within the built-up area, no open fireplaces or wood stoves are allowed. Often, during heavy fog, there is a pressing, dense air.

114094.explanation: We as Morgen! believe that the standards are already strict enough.

114095.explanation: The municipality is responsible for public spaces, the streets, the town hall, the library, and the rest is the entrepreneur's responsibility.

114100.explanation: It is good to introduce children who want to learn to music lessons.

114102.explanation: It is good that attention is given to art and culture, but we already have enough monuments. We currently have 123 national monuments, 141 municipal monuments, and 81 characteristic buildings.

114103.explanation: As a municipality, we need three enforcement officers (boa’s) given all the challenges we face.

114104.explanation: We support limited camera surveillance, such as at specific locations with specific problems, like drug trafficking and illegal dumping.

114109.explanation: We do not have holiday parks suitable for this purpose.

114110.explanation: It is good for people to work; this should also be rewarded. We know the positive stories of people who have done this. However, not everyone is capable of doing socially valuable work; look at the possibilities, not the limitations of residents.

114111.explanation: We think it is good that the municipality waives municipal taxes for residents with a low income. This ensures that everyone, regardless of financial situation, can participate in society and prevents additional financial pressure on the most vulnerable.

114112.explanation: We support maintaining the 120% social assistance norm. In our municipality, the income limit has already been raised, which is good.

114114.explanation: We have sufficient charging points for electric cars.

114115.explanation: For safety in our municipality, this is a good measure. Especially around school routes, speeding happens regularly, even within built-up areas. By strictly enforcing clear speed limits, such as a maximum of 50 km/h on main roads.

114116.explanation: It is important that everyone receives good and accessible care, but the right to care should not depend on personal preferences such as religion, culture, or beliefs. The most important thing is that care is professional, reliable, and appropriate to the medical need.

114117.explanation: It is about the price-quality ratio.

114118.explanation: It sounds very nice to lower the property tax (OZB), but the municipality is faced with increasing social challenges. Moreover, financial contributions from the national government are unpredictable, making it important to maintain a stable revenue source.

114120.explanation: We now pay €1.85 per person per night in tourist tax in 2026. The average is €2.54, so this can be well explained.

114128.explanation: Morgen! finds it important that children learn to swim well. This should be done in consultation with the schools on how to approach this.

114130.explanation: At the moment, we have more than 30% social rental housing. We would like to maintain this.

114132.explanation: In general, that is allowed, but not under all circumstances.

114133.explanation: We have a municipal parking standard; in addition, parking space near the center is important. Think of a new parking lot on Emmaweg in Geertruidenberg, among others for events.

114136.explanation: Given the limitations of the labor market, you cannot avoid working more with AI. It is essential to make this possible in a safe way to collaborate.

114137.explanation: The lines between residents and the municipality are short, which strengthens involvement and trust. Visibility and proximity are important; residents know you personally; you are a name, not a number. Cooperation on larger themes is necessary to continue existing.

114138.explanation: We have tried this before, but in the form of a youth council, it did not succeed. However, it remains important to involve young people more in politics. We will have to look together with young people at how they want to see this.

114139.explanation: We already have other housing development locations in Raamsdonk. We have cleared the 150kv high-voltage lines, but it would be nice to also look at the possibilities here.

114085.explanation: We can endorse this statement, provided that this support is largely borne by the companies involved in the energy transition, such as TenneT and RWE. The municipality also has a clear responsibility in this.

114088.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture are themselves responsible for the housing of seasonal workers on their own premises. This housing must always meet healthy, safe, and humane living environment standards.

114090.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted. With the Amercentrale as the largest 'stove' in our backyard, it is absurd to patronize residents and deny them this warmth. Municipal Interests remain in favor of freedom of choice for our residents in this matter.

114094.explanation: No. Before standards are tightened further, existing rules must be better and more consistently enforced. That provides clarity, support, and a fair balance between livability and events.

114095.explanation: No. It is not the municipality's role to financially support entrepreneurs. However, the municipality has a responsibility to actively promote accessibility and encourage entrepreneurs to do so where possible.

114100.explanation: Music lessons belong in primary education, just like physical education, we as Municipal Interests believe. This is essential for children's development. Instead of isolated subsidies, the municipality should strive to structurally reintroduce music education in primary schools.

114102.explanation: Neither, because Monument Care is essential for preserving our identity, culture, and history. The municipality's strength is not in management and operation, but in facilitating through clear subsidy policies and carefully safeguarding our cultural heritage.

114103.explanation: This topic was more relevant than ever in the past administrative period, but the coalition did not dare to make choices because it costs money. If we want to keep our municipality livable and safe, two extra enforcement officers are necessary, and camera surveillance may be needed in specific locations.

114104.explanation: Yes. Our society is changing, and safety should not be left to chance. Young and old, men and women, should feel safe and be able to walk safely. Targeted camera surveillance is possibly a necessary and responsible tool.

114109.explanation: Yes. The housing shortage calls for pragmatic solutions, including (temporarily) allowing permanent residence in holiday homes. The condition is that the park operator remains responsible for enforcement and for a tidy, safe living environment.

114110.explanation: Performing community services by people receiving social assistance can reduce the distance to the labor market. At the same time, it contributes to the community and gives people structure, development, and appreciation. This makes it a clear win-win situation.

114111.explanation: Yes. Structural waivers of municipal taxes must disappear. Instead, we should work on sustainable solutions that help people move forward without repeatedly shifting the burden to other residents.

114112.explanation: No. The current income limit for poverty schemes is sufficient. We do not need to always be the best in the class regarding provisions; these must remain affordable and responsible for all residents.

114114.explanation: No. The parking pressure is already high, partly because young people live at home longer and also get their own car. Therefore, designating additional charging spots requires careful coordination: where and how we facilitate electric charging without putting further pressure on parking space.

114115.explanation: We find a general speed reduction to 30 km/h throughout all built-up areas unnecessary. However, in residential neighborhoods, we do support 30 km/h zones aimed at traffic safety. Tailored measures are more effective than a generic one.

114116.explanation: Care must align with who someone is. People should be free to choose a healthcare provider that suits their faith, culture, or beliefs. Especially now that care is becoming increasingly expensive, freedom of choice is not a luxury but a right that must be protected.

114117.explanation: Gemeentebelangen has, through an amendment, ensured that tenders must explicitly consider profit margins. We have observed that this is insufficiently visible in practice.

114118.explanation: The possibility to lower the property tax (OZB) is strongly related to the municipality’s financial position. This is largely determined by national government contributions. Municipalities are getting more tasks from the national government while financial compensation for these tasks is under pressure. This limits policy space.

114120.explanation: Tourist tax can help cover costs related to tourism, such as maintenance of facilities and public spaces. This way tourists contribute to these costs and they are not fully passed on to our residents.

114128.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants to encourage school swimming during the opening times of outdoor swimming pools. This promotes swimming skills, safety and more exercise among young people in an accessible and positive way.

114130.explanation: Geertruidenberg meets the 30% social rental housing standard, and it should stay that way, but the region lags behind. This creates an attractive pull that is undesirable. We must also be able to provide housing for our own residents to maintain livability and identity.

114132.explanation: This is already prohibited through an umbrella zoning designation.

114133.explanation: Gemeentebelangen does not opt for a simple yes or no here. The scarce space in our centers should primarily be used for housing. Parking requires smart solutions without compromising livability and the realization of more homes.

114136.explanation: Gemeentebelangen sees AI as a tool to deploy administrative capacity more efficiently and reduce workload. AI may be supportive but never leading. The human aspect, personal contact, and careful considerations always remain central.

114137.explanation: Gemeentebelangen believes the municipality must remain independent. To maintain this, smart collaborations with other municipalities are needed without putting our autonomy under pressure. Independence is not a given but a conscious choice we want to keep making.

114138.explanation: Gemeentebelangen believes young people must structurally be able to participate in discussions and decision-making. In the past administrative period, this received insufficient attention, and even our adopted housing motion has not been executed. Young people deserve a permanent and serious voice in policy, including housing.

114139.explanation: Gemeentebelangen believes a new residential location should be investigated, with priority for our own residents for purchase and rental. Within this location, specific attention must be given to young people and the elderly, so that housing flow is promoted and suitable, smaller housing forms are realized.

114085.explanation: The municipality should stimulate this, but there is insufficient financial room for a contribution.

114088.explanation: The housing of labor migrants must be well arranged without causing nuisance to neighbors.

114090.explanation: The heating guide acts in an advisory capacity in this.

114094.explanation: Enforce existing rules. But festivals and parties should be made possible to preserve vibrancy.

114100.explanation: There are already possibilities for this through Sjors Creatief.

114102.explanation: The VVD is proud of our monuments and historic buildings. We actively commit to preserving and reusing our valuable heritage.

114103.explanation: The VVD wants more visible enforcement.

114104.explanation: At hotspots and when it contributes to safety.

114109.explanation: Not desirable and not an issue in our municipality.

114110.explanation: Everyone can contribute to society according to their capacity.

114111.explanation: Debt accumulation must be prevented. It requires tailor-made solutions.

114112.explanation: In our municipality, this is already set at 130% of the social assistance level.

114114.explanation: It comes at the expense of regular spots. The municipality must cooperate on cable ducts.

114115.explanation: This works fine in residential neighborhoods, but not on main roads. Tailored approach needed.

114116.explanation: Care must above all be effective and affordable.

114117.explanation: Otherwise care costs keep rising. Youth care must be effective and good.

114118.explanation: The property tax must not be a cash cow. Lower if possible.

114120.explanation: The VVD wants to promote tourism and recreation.

114128.explanation: Costs a lot of money and requires much time and effort from schools to organize.

114130.explanation: The percentage of social rental housing in Geertruidenberg is already above average.

114132.explanation: Already arranged in the umbrella housing plan. Nuisance must be prevented.

114136.explanation: For signage of services and working more efficiently.

114137.explanation: Preferably remain independent. Improve administrative cooperation though.

114138.explanation: Adding little value according to the VVD.

114139.explanation: With attention to the landscape, we want to explore the possibilities for this.

114085.explanation: Basically agree, but primarily and only for residents who really need financial support for this.

114088.explanation: But without exploitation or cramming too many people into one home. Living within the residential centers should also be possible.

114090.explanation: We shouldn't exaggerate too much, but reducing it would already be great.

114095.explanation: But also lead by example ourselves.

114100.explanation: Only if parents cannot afford it financially.

114103.explanation: Still a matter of being able to afford it.

114109.explanation: At least - given the current housing shortage - for the next 10 years.

114110.explanation: Volunteer work with a small allowance that is not deducted from benefits.

114112.explanation: Up to 120% to 130% of the social assistance minimum with a sliding scale, so, for example, being just €1 over does not mean losing all benefits.

114115.explanation: With exceptions for police and emergency services.

114117.explanation: Especially pay attention to the profits being made.

114118.explanation: The national government should allocate more funds to municipalities and not burden them with too many tasks.

114120.explanation: You must not price yourself out of the market.

114128.explanation: Everyone should be able to swim.

114130.explanation: Preferably 40% to 50% including senior housing. Also more flexible construction.

114132.explanation: It must be well regulated, but there is too much housing shortage to prohibit this.

114133.explanation: It is already far too crowded.

114136.explanation: Without exaggerating, ultimately human contact is always better.

114137.explanation: The larger the scale of government, the less visible the individual becomes.

114138.explanation: But all young people must be involved, not only secondary school students (HAVO - VWO), also working youth or youth in other educational tracks.

114139.explanation: There are still enough locations and opportunities to build. Let's not let this polder turn into concrete.

statements.title: Geertruidenberg must not merge with another municipality.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The use of fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Geertruidenberg.

titleParts.text: In Geertruidenberg moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should make greater use of artificial intelligence (AI).
Geertruidenberg should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
There should be more camera surveillance in Geertruidenberg.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality of Geertruidenberg should have a youth council.

titleParts.glossary: A group of young people who provide input and discuss plans and policy with the municipality.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: At least 30% of the new homes built in Geertruidenberg should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Geertruidenberg moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: Homes should be built in the Nieuwe Hooipolder, east of the A27.
